codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature scope: admin shell/header/nav/sub-nav, dashboard stats endpoint and UI, permission-aware tabs sourced from /api/auth/me, homepage placeholder at /, and unauthenticated client-side redirect to login. I found no blocking or non-blocking functional defects against the stated expectedBehavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: fullstack-worker procedure says API/UI tests should be written first (red), but this session implemented core route/layout code before creating tests while still reporting followedProcedure=true. This suggests a skill-procedure/enforcement mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md:29,42 require tests-first; worker transcript line 5 shows 'Now let me start implementing... update /api/auth/me' before later 'Now write server tests for the admin stats endpoint.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture notes describe admin protection as requireAuth + can_access_admin check, but the codebase implements this via createRequireAdmin middleware. Terminology drift can confuse future workers.

summary: Pass. The feature deliverables are present and consistent with requirements; no code-level defects were identified. I noted two shared-state documentation/procedure alignment gaps for validator triage.
